life_history: Naomi has been grappling with anxiety and guilt surrounding her return to work after a period of physical therapy for a painful condition. She has a history of feeling judged by her workplace personnel, which has led to significant dread and nervousness about interacting with HR and her manager regarding her short-term disability status. Despite having legitimate medical reasons for her absence, she often feels guilty for being away from work, which compounds her stress and anxiety.
core_belief_description: Helpless: I am helpless. I am trapped. I am out of control.
Unlovable: I am unlovable. I am bound to be alone.
intermediate_beliefs: I need to control my situation but can't.
If I don't go back to work soon, others will view me negatively.
Taking care of my health might make others judge me.
intermediate_beliefs_during_depression: I'm failing at knowing what to do to continue being out of work.
If I enjoy time away from work, it means I'm lazy and taking advantage of the system.
coping_strategies: Seeking confirmation from others about taking more time off from work.
Attempting to find clear instructions from HR policies online.
Wanting to argue against supportive comments as a means of judging self-necessity.

cognitive_models.situation: Recounting a past experience of being judged by HR for taking disability leave.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: I will always be judged negatively for tending to my health needs.
cognitive_models.behavior: Generalizes past judgement to current situation, fearing repeat scenarios, feeling mistrust towards HR.

cognitive_models.situation: Running into a coworker while not being at work due to illness.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: They're going to think I'm not really sick. I feel guilty for being out of the office.
cognitive_models.behavior: Feels nervous and stressed, excessively worries about others' perceptions even when legitimately unwell.
